title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 104:1
body: Woe waylun an expression implying chastisement to every backbiter who is a slanderer frequently engaging in backbiting and slander that is to say defamation — this was revealed regarding those who slandered the Prophet s and the believers the likes of Umayya b. Khalaf al-Walīd b. al-Mughīra and others —

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 104:2
body: who amasses read jama‘a or jamma‘a wealth and counts it over keeping count of it and stores it aside as a provision against the unforeseeable calamities of time.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 104:3
body: He thinks in his ignorance that his wealth will make him immortal never to die.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 104:4
body: Nay! — a repudiation — He will surely be flung la-yunbadhanna is the response to an omitted oath that is he will be hurled down into the Crusher that crushes everything that is thrown into it.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 104:5
body: And what will show you what will make known to you what the Crusher is?

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 104:6
body: It is the fire of God kindled set ablaze

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 104:7
body: which peers over the hearts to burn them — the pain of which is much more severe than any other because of the delicate nature of the heart.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 104:8
body: Lo! it will be closed in mu’sada or mūsada on them ‘alayhim ‘on them’ the plural pronoun takes into account the plural import of kull ‘every’

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 104:9
body: in outstretched mumaddadatin is an adjective qualifying the preceding noun ‘amadin columns read ‘umudin or ‘amadin and will therefore be inside the columns.
